    10→// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    11→// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    12→// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    13→// limitations under the License.
    14→
    15→package workspaceapi
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"errors"
    19→	"testing"
    20→	"time"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    23→)
    24→
    25→func TestMultiProcessWatcher(t *testing.T) {
    26→	t.Run("ChanProcessWatcher returns same channel", func(t *testing.T) {
    27→		ch := make(chan error)
    28→		pw := ChanProcessWatcher(ch)
    29→
    30→		got := pw.WatchProcess()
    31→		assert.Equal(t, ch, got, "WatchProcess should return the same channel")
    32→	})
    33→
    34→	t.Run("Panics on empty watchers", func(t *testing.T) {
    35→		assert.Panics(t, func() {
    36→			_ = MultiProcessWatcher()
    37→		}, "MultiProcessWatcher should panic if no watchers are provided")
    38→	})
    39→
    40→	t.Run("Returns non-nil channel", func(t *testing.T) {
    41→		wch := make(chan error, 1)
    42→		pw := ChanProcessWatcher(wch)
    43→
    44→		ret := MultiProcessWatcher(pw)
    45→		assert.NotNil(t, ret, "MultiProcessWatcher should not return nil")
    46→		assert.NotNil(t, ret.WatchProcess(), "returned watcher channel should not be nil")
    47→	})
    48→
    49→	t.Run("Propagates error to all watchers", func(t *testing.T) {
    50→		w1 := make(chan error, 1)
    51→		w2 := make(chan error, 1)
    52→
    53→		pw1 := ChanProcessWatcher(w1)
    54→		pw2 := ChanProcessWatcher(w2)
    55→
    56→		ret := MultiProcessWatcher(pw1, pw2)
    57→		retCh := ret.WatchProcess()
    58→
    59→		want := errors.New("boom")
    60→		go func() {
    61→			retCh <- want
    62→		}()
    63→
    64→		within := 200 * time.Millisecond
    65→		got1 := recvWithin(t, w1, within)
    66→		got2 := recvWithin(t, w2, within)
    67→
    68→		assert.ErrorIs(t, got1, want, "watcher 1 should receive the propagated error")
    69→		assert.ErrorIs(t, got2, want, "watcher 2 should receive the propagated error")
    70→	})
    71→
    72→	t.Run("Nil watcher channel is skipped", func(t *testing.T) {
    73→		wValid1 := make(chan error, 1)
    74→		wValid2 := make(chan error, 1)
    75→
    76→		pwValid1 := ChanProcessWatcher(wValid1)
    77→		pwValid2 := ChanProcessWatcher(wValid2)
    78→		pwNil := nilWatcher{}
    79→
    80→		ret := MultiProcessWatcher(pwNil, pwValid1, pwValid2)
    81→		retCh := ret.WatchProcess()
    82→
    83→		want := errors.New("should be delivered")
    84→		go func() {
    85→			retCh <- want
    86→		}()
    87→
    88→		within := 150 * time.Millisecond
    89→		got1 := recvWithin(t, wValid1, within)
    90→		got2 := recvWithin(t, wValid2, within)
    91→		assert.ErrorIs(t, got1, want)
    92→		assert.ErrorIs(t, got2, want)
    93→	})
    94→}
    95→
    96→func recvWithin(t *testing.T, ch <-chan error, d time.Duration) error {
    97→	t.Helper()
    98→	select {
    99→	case v := <-ch:
   100→		return v
   101→	case <-time.After(d):
   102→		assert.Fail(t, "timed out after %s waiting for receive", d.String())
   103→		return nil
   104→	}
   105→}
   106→
   107→// A ProcessWatcher that returns a nil channel (to exercise the nil-chan branch).
   108→type nilWatcher struct{}
   109→
   110→func (nilWatcher) WatchProcess() chan error { return nil }
   111→

   346→type jsonrpcMessage struct {
   347→	JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
   348→	ID      *int            `json:"id,omitempty"`
   349→	Method  string          `json:"method,omitempty"`
   350→	Params  json.RawMessage `json:"params,omitempty"`
   351→	Result  json.RawMessage `json:"result,omitempty"`
   352→	Error   *jsonrpcError   `json:"error,omitempty"`
   353→}
   354→
   355→type jsonrpcError struct {
   356→	Code    int    `json:"code"`
   357→	Message string `json:"message"`
   358→}
   359→
   360→type rpcConn struct {
   361→	mu      sync.Mutex
   362→	nextID  int
   363→	stdin   io.WriteCloser
   364→	pending map[int]chan jsonrpcMessage
   365→	closed  chan struct{}
   366→	readErr error
   367→}
   368→
   369→func newRPCConn(stdin io.WriteCloser, stdout io.ReadCloser) *rpcConn {
   370→	c := &rpcConn{
   371→		stdin:   stdin,
   372→		pending: make(map[int]chan jsonrpcMessage),
   373→		closed:  make(chan struct{}),
   374→	}
   375→	go c.readLoop(stdout)
   376→	return c
   377→}
   378→
   379→func (c *rpcConn) readLoop(r io.Reader) {
   380→	br := bufio.NewReader(r)
   381→	for {
   382→		var contentLen int
   383→		for {
   384→			line, err := br.ReadString('\n')
   385→			if err != nil {
   386→				c.mu.Lock()
   387→				c.readErr = err
   388→				for _, ch := range c.pending {
   389→					close(ch)
   390→				}
   391→				c.mu.Unlock()
   392→				close(c.closed)
   393→				return
   394→			}
   395→			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   396→			if line == "" {
   397→				break
   398→			}
   399→			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "Content-Length:") {
   400→				val := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "Content-Length:"))
   401→				contentLen, _ = strconv.Atoi(val)
   402→			}
   403→		}
   404→		if contentLen == 0 {
   405→			continue
   406→		}
   407→
   408→		body := make([]byte, contentLen)
   409→		if _, err := io.ReadFull(br, body); err != nil {
   410→			c.mu.Lock()
   411→			c.readErr = err
   412→			for _, ch := range c.pending {
   413→				close(ch)
   414→			}
   415→			c.mu.Unlock()
   416→			close(c.closed)
   417→			return
   418→		}
   419→
   420→		var msg jsonrpcMessage
   421→		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &msg); err != nil {
   422→			continue
   423→		}
   424→
   425→		if msg.ID != nil && msg.Method == "" {
   426→			c.mu.Lock()
   427→			ch, ok := c.pending[*msg.ID]
   428→			if ok {
   429→				delete(c.pending, *msg.ID)
   430→			}
   431→			c.mu.Unlock()
   432→			if ok {
   433→				ch <- msg
   434→			}
   435→		}
   436→	}
   437→}
   438→
   439→func (c *rpcConn) call(ctx context.Context, method string, params, result any) error {
   440→	c.mu.Lock()
   441→	id := c.nextID
   442→	c.nextID++
   443→	ch := make(chan jsonrpcMessage, 1)
   444→	c.pending[id] = ch
   445→	c.mu.Unlock()
   446→
   447→	paramBytes, err := json.Marshal(params)
   448→	if err != nil {
   449→		return err
   450→	}
   451→	req := jsonrpcMessage{
   452→		JSONRPC: "2.0",
   453→		ID:      &id,
   454→		Method:  method,
   455→		Params:  paramBytes,
   456→	}
   457→	body, err := json.Marshal(req)
   458→	if err != nil {
   459→		return err
   460→	}
   461→	header := fmt.Sprintf("Content-Length: %d\r\n\r\n", len(body))
   462→
   463→	c.mu.Lock()
   464→	_, err = io.WriteString(c.stdin, header)
   465→	if err == nil {
   466→		_, err = c.stdin.Write(body)
   467→	}
   468→	c.mu.Unlock()
   469→	if err != nil {
   470→		return err
   471→	}
   472→
   473→	select {
   474→	case <-ctx.Done():
   475→		return ctx.Err()
   476→	case msg, ok := <-ch:
   477→		if !ok {
   478→			return fmt.Errorf("connection closed: %v", c.readErr)
   479→		}
   480→		if msg.Error != nil {
   481→			return fmt.Errorf("rpc error %d: %s", msg.Error.Code, msg.Error.Message)
   482→		}
   483→		if result != nil && msg.Result != nil {
   484→			return json.Unmarshal(msg.Result, result)
   485→		}
   486→		return nil
   487→	}
   488→}
   489→
   490→func (c *rpcConn) notify(ctx context.Context, method string, params any) error {
   491→	paramBytes, err := json.Marshal(params)
   492→	if err != nil {
   493→		return err
   494→	}
   495→	req := jsonrpcMessage{
   496→		JSONRPC: "2.0",
   497→		Method:  method,
   498→		Params:  paramBytes,
   499→	}
   500→	body, err := json.Marshal(req)
   501→	if err != nil {
   502→		return err
   503→	}
   504→	header := fmt.Sprintf("Content-Length: %d\r\n\r\n", len(body))
   505→
   506→	c.mu.Lock()
   507→	defer c.mu.Unlock()
   508→	if _, err := io.WriteString(c.stdin, header); err != nil {
   509→		return err
   510→	}
   511→	_, err = c.stdin.Write(body)
   512→	return err
   513→}

   593→func lspPosToSemantic(p lspPosition) semanticapi.Position {
   594→	return semanticapi.Position{Line: p.Line, Character: p.Character}
   595→}
   596→
   597→func lspRangeToSemantic(r lspRange) semanticapi.Range {
   598→	return semanticapi.Range{Start: lspPosToSemantic(r.Start), End: lspPosToSemantic(r.End)}
   599→}
   600→
   601→func lspTextEditToSemantic(e lspTextEdit) semanticapi.TextEdit {
   602→	return semanticapi.TextEdit{Range: lspRangeToSemantic(e.Range), NewText: e.NewText}
   603→}
   604→
   605→func lspMarkupToSemantic(m lspMarkupContent) semanticapi.MarkupContent {
   606→	kind := semanticapi.MarkupKindPlainText
   607→	if m.Kind == "markdown" {
   608→		kind = semanticapi.MarkupKindMarkdown
   609→	}
   610→	return semanticapi.MarkupContent{Kind: kind, Value: m.Value}
   611→}
   612→
   613→func lspWorkspaceEditToSemantic(e *lspWorkspaceEdit) *semanticapi.WorkspaceEdit {
   614→	if e == nil {
   615→		return nil
   616→	}
   617→	ret := &semanticapi.WorkspaceEdit{Changes: make(map[string][]semanticapi.TextEdit)}
   618→	for uri, edits := range e.Changes {
   619→		for _, te := range edits {
   620→			ret.Changes[uri] = append(ret.Changes[uri], lspTextEditToSemantic(te))
   621→		}
   622→	}
   623→	for _, dc := range e.DocumentChanges {
   624→		uri := dc.TextDocument.URI
   625→		for _, te := range dc.Edits {
   626→			ret.Changes[uri] = append(ret.Changes[uri], lspTextEditToSemantic(te))
   627→		}
   628→	}
   629→	return ret
   630→}
   631→
   632→func lspCompletionItemToSemantic(item lspCompletionItem) semanticapi.CompletionItem {
   633→	ret := semanticapi.CompletionItem{
   634→		Label:            item.Label,
   635→		Kind:             semanticapi.CompletionItemKind(item.Kind),
   636→		Detail:           item.Detail,
   637→		InsertText:       item.InsertText,
   638→		InsertTextFormat: semanticapi.InsertTextFormat(item.InsertTextFormat),
   639→	}
   640→	if item.Documentation != nil {
   641→		var mc lspMarkupContent
   642→		if err := json.Unmarshal(item.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   643→			v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   644→			ret.Documentation = &v
   645→		} else {
   646→			var s string
   647→			if err := json.Unmarshal(item.Documentation, &s); err == nil && s != "" {
   648→				ret.Documentation = &semanticapi.MarkupContent{Kind: semanticapi.MarkupKindPlainText, Value: s}
   649→			}
   650→		}
   651→	}
   652→	if item.TextEdit != nil {
   653→		te := lspTextEditToSemantic(*item.TextEdit)
   654→		ret.TextEdit = &te
   655→	}
   656→	return ret
   657→}
   658→
   659→func lspSignatureHelpToSemantic(sh *lspSignatureHelp) *semanticapi.SignatureHelp {
   660→	if sh == nil {
   661→		return nil
   662→	}
   663→	ret := &semanticapi.SignatureHelp{
   664→		ActiveSignature: sh.ActiveSignature,
   665→		ActiveParameter: sh.ActiveParameter,
   666→	}
   667→	for _, sig := range sh.Signatures {
   668→		si := semanticapi.SignatureInformation{Label: sig.Label}
   669→		if sig.Documentation != nil {
   670→			var mc lspMarkupContent
   671→			if err := json.Unmarshal(sig.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   672→				v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   673→				si.Documentation = &v
   674→			}
   675→		}
   676→		for _, p := range sig.Parameters {
   677→			pi := semanticapi.ParameterInformation{}
   678→			// Label can be a string or [start, end] tuple.
   679→			var labelStr string
   680→			if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Label, &labelStr); err == nil {
   681→				pi.Label = labelStr
   682→			} else {
   683→				var labelArr [2]int
   684→				if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Label, &labelArr); err == nil {
   685→					pi.Label = sig.Label[labelArr[0]:labelArr[1]]
   686→				}
   687→			}
   688→			if p.Documentation != nil {
   689→				var mc lspMarkupContent
   690→				if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   691→					v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   692→					pi.Documentation = &v
   693→				}
   694→			}
   695→			si.Parameters = append(si.Parameters, pi)
   696→		}
   697→		ret.Signatures = append(ret.Signatures, si)
   698→	}
   699→	return ret
   700→}

   892→func (g *goplsLSP) CodeAction(ctx context.Context, params semanticapi.CodeActionParams) ([]semanticapi.CodeAction, error) {
   893→	diags := make([]any, len(params.Context.Diagnostics))
   894→	for i, d := range params.Context.Diagnostics {
   895→		diags[i] = map[string]any{
   896→			"range":    d.Range,
   897→			"severity": int(d.Severity),
   898→			"message":  d.Message,
   899→		}
   900→	}
   901→	p := map[string]any{
   902→		"textDocument": map[string]any{"uri": params.TextDocument.URI},
   903→		"range":        params.Range,
   904→		"context":      map[string]any{"diagnostics": diags},
   905→	}
   906→	var result []lspCodeAction
   907→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, "textDocument/codeAction", p, &result); err != nil {
   908→		return nil, err
   909→	}
   910→	ret := make([]semanticapi.CodeAction, len(result))
   911→	for i, a := range result {
   912→		ret[i] = semanticapi.CodeAction{
   913→			Title: a.Title,
   914→			Kind:  semanticapi.CodeActionKind(a.Kind),
   915→			Edit:  lspWorkspaceEditToSemantic(a.Edit),
   916→		}
   917→		if a.Command != nil {
   918→			cmd := &semanticapi.Command{
   919→				Title:   a.Command.Title,
   920→				Command: a.Command.Command,
   921→			}
   922→			cmd.Arguments = append(cmd.Arguments, a.Command.Arguments...)
   923→			ret[i].Command = cmd
   924→		}
   925→	}
   926→	return ret, nil
   927→}

  1078→func (g *goplsLSP) ExecuteCommand(ctx context.Context, params semanticapi.ExecuteCommandParams) (string, error) {
  1079→	args := make([]json.RawMessage, len(params.Arguments))
  1080→	for i, a := range params.Arguments {
  1081→		args[i] = json.RawMessage(a)
  1082→	}
  1083→	p := map[string]any{
  1084→		"command":   params.Command,
  1085→		"arguments": args,
  1086→	}
  1087→	var raw json.RawMessage
  1088→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, "workspace/executeCommand", p, &raw); err != nil {
  1089→		return "", err
  1090→	}
  1091→	if raw == nil {
  1092→		return "", nil
  1093→	}
  1094→	return string(raw), nil
  1095→}

  1251→func (g *goplsLSP) locationRequest(ctx context.Context, method string, params any) ([]semanticapi.Location, error) {
  1252→	var raw json.RawMessage
  1253→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, method, params, &raw); err != nil {
  1254→		return nil, err
  1255→	}
  1256→	if raw == nil || string(raw) == "null" {
  1257→		return nil, nil
  1258→	}
  1259→	// gopls may return a single Location or []Location.
  1260→	var locs []semanticapi.Location
  1261→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &locs); err != nil {
  1262→		var single semanticapi.Location
  1263→		if err2 := json.Unmarshal(raw, &single); err2 != nil {
  1264→			return nil, err
  1265→		}
  1266→		locs = []semanticapi.Location{single}
  1267→	}
  1268→	return locs, nil
  1269→}
  1270→
  1271→type e2eEnv struct {
  1272→	client  semanticapi.LSP
  1273→	mainURI string
  1274→	utilURI string
  1275→	stop    func()
  1276→}
  1277→
  1278→func newE2EEnv(t *testing.T) *e2eEnv {
  1279→	t.Helper()
  1280→
  1281→	goplsBin, err := exec.LookPath("gopls")
  1282→	if err != nil {
  1283→		for _, p := range []string{
  1284→			filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".rune", "bin", "gopls"),
  1285→			filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), "go", "bin", "gopls"),
  1286→		} {
  1287→			if _, err := os.Stat(p); err == nil {
  1288→				goplsBin = p
  1289→				break
  1290→			}
  1291→		}
  1292→		if goplsBin == "" {
  1293→			t.Skip("gopls not found, skipping e2e tests")
  1294→		}
  1295→	}
  1296→
  1297→	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
  1298→	testdataDir := filepath.Join("testdata")
  1299→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(testdataDir)
  1300→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1301→	for _, e := range entries {
  1302→		src := filepath.Join(testdataDir, e.Name())
  1303→		dst := filepath.Join(tmpDir, e.Name())
  1304→		data, err := os.ReadFile(src)
  1305→		require.NoError(t, err)
  1306→		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0644))
  1307→	}
  1308→
  1309→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
  1310→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, goplsBin, "serve")
  1311→	cmd.Dir = tmpDir
  1312→	stdinPipe, err := cmd.StdinPipe()
  1313→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1314→	stdoutPipe, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
  1315→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1316→	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
  1317→
  1318→	conn := newRPCConn(stdinPipe, stdoutPipe)
  1319→	adapter := &goplsLSP{conn: conn}
  1320→
  1321→	n := sockCounter.Add(1)
  1322→	sock := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), fmt.Sprintf("lsp_e2e_%d_%d.sock", os.Getpid(), n))
  1323→	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.Remove(sock) })
  1324→	lis, err := net.Listen("unix", sock)
  1325→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1326→
  1327→	srv := grpc.NewServer()
  1328→	semanticrpc.RegisterLSPServer(srv, semanticrpc.NewServer(adapter))
  1329→	go func() { _ = srv.Serve(lis) }()
  1330→
  1331→	cc, err := grpc.NewClient(
  1332→		"passthrough:///unix://"+sock,
  1333→		grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),
  1334→		grpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
  1335→			var d net.Dialer
  1336→			return d.DialContext(ctx, "unix", sock)
  1337→		}),
  1338→	)
  1339→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1340→
  1341→	client := semanticrpc.NewClient(context.Background(), cc)
  1342→
  1343→	rootURI := "file://" + tmpDir
  1344→	mainURI := "file://" + filepath.Join(tmpDir, "main.go")
  1345→	utilURI := "file://" + filepath.Join(tmpDir, "util.go")
  1346→
  1347→	_, err = client.Initialize(ctx, semanticapi.InitializeParams{RootURI: rootURI})
  1348→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1349→	require.NoError(t, client.Initialized(ctx))
  1350→
  1351→	mainContent, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(tmpDir, "main.go"))
  1352→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1353→	utilContent, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(tmpDir, "util.go"))
  1354→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1355→
  1356→	require.NoError(t, client.DidOpen(ctx, semanticapi.DidOpenTextDocumentParams{
  1357→		TextDocument: semanticapi.TextDocumentItem{
  1358→			URI: mainURI, LanguageID: "go", Version: 1, Text: string(mainContent),
  1359→		},
  1360→	}))
  1361→	require.NoError(t, client.DidOpen(ctx, semanticapi.DidOpenTextDocumentParams{
  1362→		TextDocument: semanticapi.TextDocumentItem{
  1363→			URI: utilURI, LanguageID: "go", Version: 1, Text: string(utilContent),
  1364→		},
  1365→	}))
  1366→
  1367→	time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
  1368→
  1369→	stop := func() {
  1370→		_ = client.Shutdown(context.Background())
  1371→		_ = client.Exit(context.Background())
  1372→		_ = cc.Close()
  1373→		srv.GracefulStop()
  1374→		cancel()
  1375→		_ = cmd.Wait()
  1376→	}
  1377→
  1378→	return &e2eEnv{
  1379→		client:  client,
  1380→		mainURI: mainURI,
  1381→		utilURI: utilURI,
  1382→		stop:    stop,
  1383→	}
  1384→}
  1385→

   363→// CompletionItem represents a completion entry.

   376→// MarshalJSON implements custom JSON marshaling for CompletionItem.
   377→// The "documentation" field is either a string or a MarkupContent object per LSP spec.
   378→func (c CompletionItem) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
   379→	type plain CompletionItem
   380→	type withDoc struct {
   381→		plain
   382→		Documentation json.RawMessage `json:"documentation,omitempty"`
   383→	}
   384→	w := withDoc{plain: plain(c)}
   385→	if c.Documentation != nil {
   386→		b, err := json.Marshal(c.Documentation)
   387→		if err != nil {
   388→			return nil, err
   389→		}
   390→		w.Documentation = b
   391→	} else if c.DocumentationString != "" {
   392→		b, err := json.Marshal(c.DocumentationString)
   393→		if err != nil {
   394→			return nil, err
   395→		}
   396→		w.Documentation = b
   397→	}
   398→	return json.Marshal(w)
   399→}

   401→// UnmarshalJSON implements custom JSON unmarshaling for CompletionItem.
   402→func (c *CompletionItem) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
   403→	type plain CompletionItem
   404→	type withDoc struct {
   405→		plain
   406→		Documentation json.RawMessage `json:"documentation,omitempty"`
   407→	}
   408→	var w withDoc
   409→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &w); err != nil {
   410→		return err
   411→	}
   412→	*c = CompletionItem(w.plain)
   413→	if len(w.Documentation) > 0 {
   414→		if w.Documentation[0] == '{' {
   415→			var mc MarkupContent
   416→			if err := json.Unmarshal(w.Documentation, &mc); err == nil {
   417→				c.Documentation = &mc
   418→				return nil
   419→			}
   420→		}
   421→		var s string
   422→		if err := json.Unmarshal(w.Documentation, &s); err == nil {
   423→			c.DocumentationString = s
   424→		}
   425→	}
   426→	return nil
   427→}

   448→// MarshalJSON implements custom JSON marshaling for ParameterInformation.

   450→func (p ParameterInformation) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
   451→	type withLabel struct {

   453→		Documentation *MarkupContent  `json:"documentation,omitempty"`
   454→	}
   455→	w := withLabel{Documentation: p.Documentation}
   456→	if p.LabelOffsets != nil {
   457→		b, err := json.Marshal(p.LabelOffsets)
   458→		if err != nil {
   459→			return nil, err
   460→		}
   461→		w.Label = b
   462→	} else {
   463→		b, err := json.Marshal(p.Label)
   464→		if err != nil {
   465→			return nil, err
   466→		}
   467→		w.Label = b
   468→	}
   469→	return json.Marshal(w)
   470→}
   471→
   472→// UnmarshalJSON implements custom JSON unmarshaling for ParameterInformation.
   473→func (p *ParameterInformation) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
   474→	type withLabel struct {
   475→		Label         json.RawMessage `json:"label"`
   476→		Documentation *MarkupContent  `json:"documentation,omitempty"`
   477→	}
   478→	var w withLabel
   479→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &w); err != nil {
   480→		return err
   481→	}
   482→	p.Documentation = w.Documentation
   483→	if len(w.Label) > 0 && w.Label[0] == '[' {
   484→		var offsets [2]uint32
   485→		if err := json.Unmarshal(w.Label, &offsets); err == nil {
   486→			p.LabelOffsets = &offsets
   487→			return nil
   488→		}
   489→	}
   490→	return json.Unmarshal(w.Label, &p.Label)
   491→}

   791→// MarshalJSON implements custom JSON marshaling for InlayHint.
   792→// The "label" field is either a string or an array of InlayHintLabelPart per LSP spec.
   793→func (h InlayHint) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
   794→	type plain InlayHint
   795→	type withLabel struct {
   796→		plain
   797→		Label json.RawMessage `json:"label"`
   798→	}
   799→	w := withLabel{plain: plain(h)}
   800→	if len(h.LabelParts) > 0 {
   801→		b, err := json.Marshal(h.LabelParts)
   802→		if err != nil {
   803→			return nil, err
   804→		}
   805→		w.Label = b
   806→	} else {
   807→		b, err := json.Marshal(h.Label)
   808→		if err != nil {
   809→			return nil, err
   810→		}
   811→		w.Label = b
   812→	}
   813→	return json.Marshal(w)
   814→}
   815→
   816→// UnmarshalJSON implements custom JSON unmarshaling for InlayHint.
   817→func (h *InlayHint) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
   818→	type plain InlayHint
   819→	type withLabel struct {
   820→		plain
   821→		Label json.RawMessage `json:"label"`
   822→	}
   823→	var w withLabel
   824→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &w); err != nil {
   825→		return err
   826→	}
   827→	*h = InlayHint(w.plain)
   828→	if len(w.Label) > 0 && w.Label[0] == '[' {
   829→		return json.Unmarshal(w.Label, &h.LabelParts)
   830→	}
   831→	return json.Unmarshal(w.Label, &h.Label)
   832→}

   889→// UnmarshalJSON implements custom JSON unmarshaling for ServerCapabilities.
   890→// LSP allows some provider fields to be either bool or options object.
   891→func (c *ServerCapabilities) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
   892→	// Use a raw map to handle bool | object union types.
   893→	var raw map[string]json.RawMessage
   894→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &raw); err != nil {
   895→		return err
   896→	}
   897→
   898→	unmarshalBool := func(key string) bool {
   899→		v, ok := raw[key]
   900→		if !ok {
   901→			return false
   902→		}
   903→		var b bool
   904→		if json.Unmarshal(v, &b) == nil {
   905→			return b
   906→		}
   907→		// If it's an object, it's truthy.
   908→		return v[0] == '{'
   909→	}
   910→
   911→	// unmarshalBoolOrOptions: if true → &T{}, if object → unmarshal, if false/absent → nil.
   912→	unmarshalPtr := func(key string, target any) {
   913→		v, ok := raw[key]
   914→		if !ok || string(v) == "false" || string(v) == "null" {
   915→			return
   916→		}
   917→		if string(v) == "true" {
   918→			// target is already zero-valued pointer; caller handles.
   919→			return
   920→		}
   921→		_ = json.Unmarshal(v, target)
   922→	}
   923→
   924→	unmarshalPtrOrBool := func(key string, alloc func() any) any {
   925→		v, ok := raw[key]
   926→		if !ok || string(v) == "false" || string(v) == "null" {
   927→			return nil
   928→		}
   929→		ptr := alloc()
   930→		if string(v) == "true" {
   931→			return ptr
   932→		}
   933→		_ = json.Unmarshal(v, ptr)
   934→		return ptr
   935→	}
   936→
   937→	c.HoverProvider = unmarshalBool("hoverProvider")
   938→	c.DeclarationProvider = unmarshalBool("declarationProvider")
   939→	c.DefinitionProvider = unmarshalBool("definitionProvider")
   940→	c.TypeDefinitionProvider = unmarshalBool("typeDefinitionProvider")
   941→	c.ImplementationProvider = unmarshalBool("implementationProvider")
   942→	c.ReferencesProvider = unmarshalBool("referencesProvider")
   943→	c.DocumentHighlightProvider = unmarshalBool("documentHighlightProvider")
   944→	c.DocumentSymbolProvider = unmarshalBool("documentSymbolProvider")
   945→	c.DocumentFormattingProvider = unmarshalBool("documentFormattingProvider")
   946→	c.DocumentRangeFormattingProvider = unmarshalBool("documentRangeFormattingProvider")
   947→	c.FoldingRangeProvider = unmarshalBool("foldingRangeProvider")
   948→	c.SelectionRangeProvider = unmarshalBool("selectionRangeProvider")
   949→	c.WorkspaceSymbolProvider = unmarshalBool("workspaceSymbolProvider")
   950→	c.CallHierarchyProvider = unmarshalBool("callHierarchyProvider")
   951→	c.DocumentColorProvider = unmarshalBool("documentColorProvider")
   952→	c.LinkedEditingRangeProvider = unmarshalBool("linkedEditingRangeProvider")
   953→	c.MonikerProvider = unmarshalBool("monikerProvider")
   954→	c.TypeHierarchyProvider = unmarshalBool("typeHierarchyProvider")
   955→	c.InlineValueProvider = unmarshalBool("inlineValueProvider")
   956→
   957→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("completionProvider", func() any { return &CompletionOptions{} }); p != nil {
   958→		c.CompletionProvider = p.(*CompletionOptions)
   959→	}
   960→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("signatureHelpProvider", func() any { return &SignatureHelpOptions{} }); p != nil {
   961→		c.SignatureHelpProvider = p.(*SignatureHelpOptions)
   962→	}
   963→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("codeActionProvider", func() any { return &CodeActionOptions{} }); p != nil {
   964→		c.CodeActionProvider = p.(*CodeActionOptions)
   965→	}
   966→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("codeLensProvider", func() any { return &CodeLensOptions{} }); p != nil {
   967→		c.CodeLensProvider = p.(*CodeLensOptions)
   968→	}
   969→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("renameProvider", func() any { return &RenameOptions{} }); p != nil {
   970→		c.RenameProvider = p.(*RenameOptions)
   971→	}
   972→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("semanticTokensProvider", func() any { return &SemanticTokensOptions{} }); p != nil {
   973→		c.SemanticTokensProvider = p.(*SemanticTokensOptions)
   974→	}
   975→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("inlayHintProvider", func() any { return &InlayHintOptions{} }); p != nil {
   976→		c.InlayHintProvider = p.(*InlayHintOptions)
   977→	}
   978→
   979→	// These are always objects, never bools.
   980→	c.ExecuteCommandProvider = nil
   981→	unmarshalPtr("executeCommandProvider", &c.ExecuteCommandProvider)
   982→	c.DiagnosticProvider = nil
   983→	unmarshalPtr("diagnosticProvider", &c.DiagnosticProvider)
   984→	c.DocumentLinkProvider = nil
   985→	if p := unmarshalPtrOrBool("documentLinkProvider", func() any { return &DocumentLinkOptions{} }); p != nil {
   986→		c.DocumentLinkProvider = p.(*DocumentLinkOptions)
   987→	}
   988→	c.DocumentOnTypeFormattingProvider = nil
   989→	unmarshalPtr("documentOnTypeFormattingProvider", &c.DocumentOnTypeFormattingProvider)
   990→
   991→	return nil
   992→}

    30→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi"
    31→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi/docmarshal/doctoml"
    32→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi/storagerpc"
    33→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/syntaxapi"
    34→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/syntaxapi/syntaxrpc"
    35→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/textapi"
    36→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/textapi/textrpc"
    37→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/workspaceapi"
    38→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/workspaceapi/workspacerpc"
    39→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/term"
    40→	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
    41→	"google.golang.org/grpc"
    42→	"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials"
    43→	"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure"
    44→	"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/oauth"
    45→)
    46→
    47→// Workspace abstracts resources associated with a Workspace.
    48→type Workspace struct {
    49→	dataDir string
    50→	conn    grpc.ClientConnInterface
    51→	editor  *textrpc.Client
    52→	config  config.Config
    53→	meta    Metadata
    54→}
    55→
    56→// DataDir returns the data directory to store data to re-use across sessions.
    57→func (w *Workspace) DataDir(ctx context.Context) string {
    58→	return w.dataDir
    59→}
    60→
    61→// WindowManager returns the workspace's window manager, which can be used
    62→// to create and manipulate the workspace's browser tabs, windows and bars.
    63→func (w *Workspace) WindowManager(ctx context.Context) browserapi.WindowManager {
    64→	return browserrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    65→}
    66→
    67→// ResourceOpener returns the workspace's resource opener which allows
    68→// clients to access resources in the workspace. Extensions that need more control
    69→// over the underlying files can use FileSystem instead.
    70→func (w *Workspace) ResourceOpener(ctx context.Context) browserapi.ResourceOpener {
    71→	return browserrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    72→}
    73→
    74→// Notifications returns the workspace's notifications.
    75→func (w *Workspace) Notifications(ctx context.Context) browserapi.Notifications {
    76→	return browserrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    77→}
    78→
    79→// FileSystem returns the workspace's file system, which can be used to
    80→// directly manipulate raw files in the workspace. Extensions that
    81→// want to simply open resources as tabs in a workspace should use ResourceOpener.
    82→func (w *Workspace) FileSystem(ctx context.Context) workspaceapi.FileSystem {
    83→	return workspacerpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    84→}
    85→
    86→// Executor returns the workspace's executor, which can be used
    87→// to start and stop processes.
    88→func (w *Workspace) Executor(ctx context.Context) workspaceapi.Executor {
    89→	return workspacerpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    90→}
    91→
    92→// Terminal returns the workspace's pty capability which can be used to
    93→// implement a terminal.
    94→func (w *Workspace) Terminal(ctx context.Context) workspaceapi.Terminal {
    95→	return workspacerpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
    96→}
    97→
    98→// Editor returns the workspace's editor which can be used to monitor
    99→// and edit files, move the cursor, add location lists with or without
   100→// color attributes, and even set the default background and foreground
   101→// of a resource.
   102→func (w *Workspace) Editor(ctx context.Context) textapi.Editor {
   103→	return textrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
   104→}
   105→
   106→// LSP returns the workspace's LSP client, which can be used to interact
   107→// with language servers.
   108→func (w *Workspace) LSP(ctx context.Context) semanticapi.LSP {
   109→	return semanticrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
   110→}
   111→
   112→// RegisterCommand registers a new command which is to be dispatched to the
   113→// given CommandHandler. This is the primary mechanism extensions should use
   114→// to run new functionality.
   115→func (w *Workspace) RegisterCommand(
   116→	cmd textapi.CommandManual, h textapi.CommandHandler,
   117→) error {
   118→	return w.editorClient(context.Background()).SubscribeCommand(cmd, h)
   119→}
   120→
   121→// Searcher returns the workspace's syntax searcher, which can be used
   122→// to perform AST-level searches across workspace files.
   123→func (w *Workspace) Searcher(ctx context.Context) syntaxapi.Searcher {
   124→	return syntaxrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
   125→}
   126→
   127→// Storage returns the workspace's storage facility, which can be used to
   128→// persist extension data across sessions.
   129→func (w *Workspace) Storage(ctx context.Context) storageapi.Service {
   130→	c := new(storagerpc.Client)
   131→	c.Init(w.conn, doctoml.Marshaler())
   132→	return storageapi.WithPartition(c, w.meta.ExtensionID)
   133→}
   134→
   135→// Interrupter returns the workspace's event loop interrupter, which
   136→// can be used to request new draws when state changes asynchronously.
   137→func (w *Workspace) Interrupter(ctx context.Context) term.Interrupter {
   138→	return browserrpc.NewClient(ctx, w.conn)
   139→}
   140→
   141→// Config returns a copy of the workspace's global configuration.
   142→func (w *Workspace) Config(ctx context.Context) config.Config {
   143→	if w.config == nil {
   144→		c, err := configrpc.FetchConfig(w.conn)
   145→		if err != nil {
   146→			return config.ErrConfig(fmt.Errorf("fetch config: %w", err))
   147→		}
   148→		w.config = c
   149→	}
   150→	return w.config
   151→}
   152→
   153→// RawConn returns the underlying raw connection to the extension host.
   154→// This method should generally not be used by extensions.
   155→func (w *Workspace) RawConn() grpc.ClientConnInterface {
   156→	return w.conn
   157→}
   158→
   159→// NewWorkspace returns a new Workspace. Extensions should use
   160→// ServeWorkspaceExtension, which performs the stdin/stdout exchange
   161→// necessary to receive a valid Config.
   162→func NewWorkspace(req Config, meta Metadata) (*Workspace, error) {
   163→	ret := new(Workspace)
   164→	ret.dataDir = req.DataDir
   165→	opts := []grpc.DialOption{
   166→		grpc.WithContextDialer(
   167→			func(ctx context.Context, _ string) (net.Conn, error) {
   168→				addr, err := net.ResolveUnixAddr("unix", req.Socket)
   169→				if err != nil {
   170→					return nil, err
   171→				}
   172→				var d net.Dialer
   173→				return d.DialContext(ctx, addr.Network(), addr.String())
   174→			},
   175→		)}
   176→
   177→	if len(req.Certificate) != 0 {
   178→		certPool := x509.NewCertPool()
   179→		if !certPool.AppendCertsFromPEM(req.Certificate) {
   180→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("credentials: failed to append certificates")
   181→		}
   182→		tlsCfg := tls.Config{InsecureSkipVerify: true, RootCAs: certPool}
   183→		transportCreds := credentials.NewTLS(&tlsCfg)
   184→		opts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(transportCreds))
   185→	} else {
   186→		opts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	if req.Token != nil {
   190→		tokenSource := oauth2.StaticTokenSource(req.Token)
   191→		rpcCreds := oauth.TokenSource{TokenSource: tokenSource}
   192→		opts = append(opts, grpc.WithPerRPCCredentials(rpcCreds))
   193→	}
   194→
   195→	// passthrough is used to indicate that dns
   196→	// resolution SHOULD NOT be performed on target
   197→	conn, err := grpc.NewClient("passthrough:", opts...)
   198→	if err != nil {
   199→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("dial: %w", err)
   200→	}
   201→	ret.conn = conn
   202→	ret.meta = meta
   203→	return ret, nil
   204→}
   205→
   206→func (w *Workspace) editorClient(ctx context.Context) *textrpc.Client {
   207→	if w.editor == nil {
   208→		w.editor = w.Editor(ctx).(*textrpc.Client)
   209→	}
   210→	return w.editor
   211→}
   212→

    10→// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    11→// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    12→// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    13→// limitations under the License.
    14→
    15→package comptest
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"fmt"
    19→	"strings"
    20→	"testing"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    23→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/term"
    24→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/tui"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// TestCase represents an action and how a component
    28→// is expected to be drawn after this action.
    29→type TestCase struct {
    30→	Action   func()
    31→	Expected string
    32→}
    33→
    34→// StringerWriter is a term.Writer that also is able to produce a string screen output.
    35→type StringerWriter interface {
    36→	term.Writer
    37→	fmt.Stringer
    38→	Flush() error
    39→	Clear(attr term.Attributes) (err error)
    40→}
    41→
    42→// TestComponent tests a given component against a set of ComponentTestCase.
    43→func TestComponent(
    44→	t *testing.T, m tui.Component,
    45→	w StringerWriter, cases []TestCase,
    46→) {
    47→	t.Helper()
    48→	var err error
    49→
    50→	for i, tcase := range cases {
    51→		t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("test case %d", i), func(t *testing.T) {
    52→			if err = w.Clear(term.Attributes{}); err != nil {
    53→				t.Fatal(err)
    54→			}
    55→
    56→			if tcase.Action != nil {
    57→				tcase.Action()
    58→			}
    59→
    60→			m.Draw(w)
    61→
    62→			if err := w.Flush(); err != nil {
    63→				t.Fatal(err)
    64→			}
    65→
    66→			// for readability, we expected strings are written starting with \n
    67→			expected := strings.TrimLeft(tcase.Expected, "\n")
    68→			assert.Equal(t, expected, w.String(), "testcase %d failed", i)
    69→		})
    70→	}
    71→}
    72→

    13→// limitations under the License.
    14→
    15→package handlertest
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"fmt"
    19→	"testing"
    20→
    21→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    22→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    23→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/term"
    24→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/tui"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// SequenceTestCase represents an input sequence and
    28→// the result expected draw string representation.
    29→type SequenceTestCase struct {
    30→	InputSequence string
    31→	Expected      string
    32→}
    33→
    34→// SingleTestCase represents an event and the result
    35→// expected draw string representation.
    36→type SingleTestCase struct {
    37→	Event    term.Event
    38→	Expected string
    39→}
    40→
    41→// DrawHandler is a helper function that renders the handler into a string.
    42→//
    43→// It can be beneficial for print-debugging your tests:
    44→//
    45→// fmt.Printf("\n%s", handlertest.DrawHandler(vi, 20, 20))
    46→func DrawHandler(handler tui.Handler, width, height int) string {
    47→	w := term.NewStringWriter(width, height)
    48→	handler.Draw(w)
    49→	cursor, _, ok := handler.Cursor()
    50→	if ok {
    51→		w.SetCursor(cursor)
    52→	}
    53→	_ = w.Flush()
    54→	out := w.String()
    55→	return out
    56→}
    57→
    58→// RunHandlerIsolated is a helper function that runs a set of test cases,
    59→// by calling fn for every test case, resizing the given handler with
    60→// the given width and height and comparing the results of Draw against it.
    61→func RunHandlerIsolated(
    62→	t *testing.T, fn func(t *testing.T) tui.Handler, width, height int,
    63→	cases []SequenceTestCase,
    64→) {
    65→	writer := term.NewStringWriter(width, height)
    66→
    67→	for i, tcase := range cases {
    68→		t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("test case %d (input: %s)", i, tcase.InputSequence),
    69→			func(t *testing.T) {
    70→				handler := fn(t)
    71→				handler.Resize(width, height)
    72→				runTestCase(t, i, writer, handler, tcase)
    73→			})
    74→	}
    75→}
    76→
    77→// RunHandlerSequenceWriter is a helper function that runs a set of test cases
    78→// against the given handler in sequence.
    79→func RunHandlerSequenceWriter(
    80→	t *testing.T, writer *term.StringWriter, handler tui.Handler, width, height int,
    81→	cases []SequenceTestCase,
    82→) {
    83→	handler.Resize(width, height)
    84→	for i, tcase := range cases {
    85→		runTestCase(t, i, writer, handler, tcase)
    86→	}
    87→}
    88→
    89→// RunHandlerSequence is a helper function that runs a set of test cases
    90→// against the given handler in sequence, with a StringWriter set with the given
    91→// width and height.
    92→func RunHandlerSequence(
    93→	t *testing.T, handler tui.Handler, width, height int,
    94→	cases []SequenceTestCase,
    95→) {
    96→	writer := term.NewStringWriter(width, height)
    97→	RunHandlerSequenceWriter(t, writer, handler, width, height, cases)
    98→}
    99→
   100→func runTestCase(
   101→	t *testing.T, i int, w *term.StringWriter,
   102→	h tui.Handler, tcase SequenceTestCase,
   103→) {
   104→	err := w.Clear(term.Attributes{Fg: 0, Bg: 0})
   105→	require.NoError(t, err)
   106→
   107→	keys, err := term.ParseKeys(tcase.InputSequence)
   108→	require.NoError(t, err)
   109→
   110→	for _, key := range keys {
   111→		h.Handle(term.Event{Ch: key.Ch, Mod: key.Mod, Key: key.Key, Type: term.EventKey})
   112→	}
   113→	h.Draw(w)
   114→
   115→	cursor, _, ok := h.Cursor()
   116→	if ok {
   117→		w.SetCursor(cursor)
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	err = w.Flush()
   121→	require.NoError(t, err)
   122→
   123→	out := w.String()
   124→	assert.Equal(t, tcase.Expected, out, "test case %d (input: %s)", i, tcase.InputSequence)
   125→}
   126→

    14→package storagestub
    15→
    16→import (
    17→	"context"
    18→	"reflect"
    19→	"sync"
    20→
    21→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi"
    22→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi/docmarshal"
    23→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/api/storageapi/docmarshal/docbson"
    24→)
    25→
    26→type inMemoryService struct {
    27→	marshaler docmarshal.Marshaler
    28→	m         sync.Locker
    29→	storage   map[string][]byte
    30→}
    31→
    32→// NewInMemoryService returns an instance of storageapi.Service backed
    33→// by an in-memory map, and the default bson marshaler.
    34→func NewInMemoryService() storageapi.DroppableService {
    35→	return NewInMemoryServiceWithMarshaler(docbson.Marshaler())
    36→}
    37→
    38→// NewInMemoryServiceWithMarshaler returns a in-memory service powered
    39→// by the given marshaler.
    40→func NewInMemoryServiceWithMarshaler(m docmarshal.Marshaler) storageapi.DroppableService {
    41→	return &inMemoryService{
    42→		marshaler: m,
    43→		m:         new(sync.Mutex),
    44→		storage:   make(map[string][]byte),
    45→	}
    46→}
    47→
    48→func (c *inMemoryService) Set(
    49→	ctx context.Context, ID string, data interface{},
    50→) error {
    51→	return c.set(ctx, ID, data, false)
    52→}
    53→
    54→func (c *inMemoryService) Create(
    55→	ctx context.Context, ID string, data interface{},
    56→) error {
    57→	return c.set(ctx, ID, data, true)
    58→}
    59→
    60→func (c *inMemoryService) set(
    61→	ctx context.Context, ID string, data interface{},
    62→	errAlreadyExists bool,
    63→) (err error) {
    64→	if data == nil {
    65→		panic("invalid nil data argument to Create/Set")
    66→	}
    67→	data, err = DerefCreateValue(reflect.ValueOf(data))
    68→	if err != nil {
    69→		return
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	c.m.Lock()
    73→	defer c.m.Unlock()
    74→
    75→	if errAlreadyExists {
    76→		_, ok := c.storage[ID]
    77→		if ok {
    78→			return storageapi.ErrAlreadyExists
    79→		}
    80→	}
    81→	c.storage[ID] = Encode(c.marshaler, data, true)
    82→	return
    83→}
    84→
    85→func (c *inMemoryService) getValue(ID string, doc interface{}) (
    86→	err error,
    87→) {
    88→	var ok bool
    89→
    90→	var raw []byte
    91→	raw, ok = c.storage[ID]
    92→	if !ok {
    93→		err = storageapi.ErrNotFound
    94→		return
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	return SafeDecode(c.marshaler, doc, raw)
    98→}
    99→
   100→func (c *inMemoryService) Get(
   101→	ctx context.Context, ID string, to interface{},
   102→) (err error) {
   103→	c.m.Lock()
   104→	defer c.m.Unlock()
   105→
   106→	err = c.getValue(ID, to)
   107→	return
   108→}
   109→
   110→func (c *inMemoryService) Update(
   111→	ctx context.Context, ID string, updates []storageapi.Update,
   112→	preconds ...storageapi.Precondition,
   113→) error {
   114→	if len(updates) == 0 {
   115→		panic("Update: no paths to update")
   116→	}
   117→
   118→	c.m.Lock()
   119→	defer c.m.Unlock()
   120→
   121→	var proto map[string]interface{}
   122→	err := c.getValue(ID, &proto)
   123→	if err != nil {
   124→		return err
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	err = UpdateProto(c.marshaler, updates, proto, preconds...)
   128→	if err != nil {
   129→		return err
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	c.storage[ID] = Encode(c.marshaler, proto, false)
   133→
   134→	return nil
   135→}
   136→
   137→func (c *inMemoryService) Close() error {
   138→	return nil
   139→}
   140→
   141→func (c *inMemoryService) Delete(ctx context.Context, ID string) error {
   142→	c.m.Lock()
   143→	defer c.m.Unlock()
   144→
   145→	delete(c.storage, ID)
   146→	return nil
   147→}
   148→
   149→func (c *inMemoryService) List(ctx context.Context, filters []storageapi.Filter) (
   150→	it storageapi.Iterator, err error,
   151→) {
   152→	iter := NewListIterator(c.marshaler)
   153→
   154→	c.m.Lock()
   155→	defer c.m.Unlock()
   156→
   157→	for _, v := range c.storage {
   158→		iter.Extend(filters, v)
   159→	}
   160→
   161→	return iter, nil
   162→}
   163→
   164→func (c *inMemoryService) Drop(ctx context.Context) error {
   165→	c.m.Lock()
   166→	defer c.m.Unlock()
   167→
   168→	c.storage = make(map[string][]byte)
   169→	return nil
   170→}
   171→

70→	// WatchProcess returns a channel that can be used to
    71→	// wait for the command to exit and waits for any copying to stdin or
    72→	// copying from stdout or stderr to complete.
    73→
    74→	// The returned error is nil if the command runs, has no problems copying
    75→	// stdin, stdout, and stderr, and exits with a zero exit status.
    76→	WatchProcess() chan error
    77→}
    78→
    79→// Executor is the public facing API of a workspace's command execution.
    80→type Executor interface {
    81→	// Start starts the given cmd and returns the Pid of the underlying
    82→	// process.
    83→	Start(context.Context, Cmd) (Pid, error)
    84→
    85→	// Signal sends a signal to the running process.
    86→	Signal(Pid, syscall.Signal) error
    87→
    88→	io.Closer
    89→}
    90→
    91→// Terminal abstracts the ability to manage pseudoterminals.
    92→type Terminal interface {
    93→	// StartPty creates a new pseudoterminal.
    94→	StartPty() (Pty, error)
    95→
    96→	// SetPtySize sets the width and height in columns and rows of
    97→	// a pseudoterminal.
    98→	SetPtySize(p Pty, width, height int) error
    99→}
   100→
   101→// Pty is a pseudoterminal on a Workspace.
   102→type Pty struct {
   103→	// Master is the pty master device file.
   104→	Master File
   105→	// Slave is the pseudoterminal slave device path.
   106→	Slave File
   107→}
   108→
   109→// File abstracts a subset of os.File.
   110→type File interface {
   111→	Name() string

   113→	Sync() error
   114→	Truncate(size int64) error

    14→
    15→package config
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"testing"
    19→	"time"
    20→
    21→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    22→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    23→	"github.com/unstablebuild/tcell/v3"
    24→)
    25→
    26→func TestConfigOk(t *testing.T) {
    27→	assert.Panics(t, func() {
    28→		MapConfig(nil)
    29→	})
    30→
    31→	c := MapConfig(make(map[string]interface{}))
    32→
    33→	_, err := c.GetString("mk")
    34→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    35→
    36→	_, err = c.GetBool("mk")
    37→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    38→
    39→	_, err = c.GetConfig("mk")
    40→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    41→
    42→	_, err = c.GetFloat("mk")
    43→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    44→
    45→	_, err = c.GetInt("mk")
    46→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    47→
    48→	_, err = c.GetAttribute("mk")
    49→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    50→
    51→	_, err = GetAttributes(c, "mk")
    52→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    53→
    54→	_, err = c.GetRune("mk")
    55→	assert.Equal(t, ErrNotFound, err)
    56→}
    57→
    58→func TestConfigTypes(t *testing.T) {
    59→	m := map[string]interface{}{
    60→		"a1":     rune('a'),
    61→		"a2":     byte('a'),
    62→		"a3":     string('a'),
    63→		"a4":     []byte{'a'},
    64→		"a5":     []rune{'a'},
    65→		"11":     1,
    66→		"12":     int(1),
    67→		"13":     int32(1),
    68→		"14":     int64(1),
    69→		"15":     float64(1),
    70→		"16":     float32(1),
    71→		"21":     2.1,
    72→		"22":     float64(2.1),
    73→		"23":     float32(2.1),
    74→		"true":   true,
    75→		"false":  false,
    76→		"attr_1": map[string]interface{}{"fg": "blue", "bg": "#f0f0f0"},
    77→		"attr_2": map[string]interface{}{"fg": "red", "flags": []interface{}{"bold", "italic"}},
    78→		"attr_3": map[string]interface{}{"fg": "red", "flag": []interface{}{"bold", "italic"}},
    79→		"charset_1": map[string]interface{}{"topleft": "a",
    80→			"topright": "b", "bottomleft": "c",
    81→			"bottomright": "d", "horizontalbottom": "e", "verticalleft": "f"},
    82→		"charset_2":  map[string]interface{}{"topleft": 'a'},
    83→		"duration_1": "2s",
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	tsuite := []struct {
    87→		desc string
    88→		cfg  Config
    89→	}{
    90→		{"MapConfig", MapConfig(m)},
    91→		{"Clone", Clone(MapConfig(m))},
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	for _, tcase := range tsuite {
    95→		t.Run(tcase.desc, func(t *testing.T) {
    96→			m := make(map[string]interface{})
    97→			tcase.cfg.Iterate(func(k string, v interface{}) {
    98→				m[k] = v
    99→			})
   100→			c1 := MapConfig(m)
   101→			c2 := MapConfig(map[string]interface{}{
   102→				"nested": m,
   103→			})
   104→
   105→			cnested, err := c2.GetConfig("nested")
   106→			require.Nil(t, err)
   107→
   108→			// exceptions for unmarshaled
   109→			m["a1"] = "a"
   110→			m["a2"] = "a"
   111→			m["a4"] = "a"
   112→			m["a5"] = "a"
   113→			j1 := JSON{mapConfig(m)}
   114→			data, err := j1.MarshalText()
   115→			require.NoError(t, err)
   116→
   117→			var j2 JSON
   118→			err = j2.UnmarshalText(data)
   119→			require.NoError(t, err)
   120→			cunmarshaled := j2.mapConfig
   121→
   122→			suite := []struct {
   123→				description string
   124→				Config
   125→			}{
   126→				{"simple", c1},
   127→				{"nested", cnested},
   128→				{"unmarshaled", cunmarshaled},
   129→			}
   130→
   131→			for _, tcase := range suite {
   132→				c := tcase.Config
   133→				t.Run(tcase.description, func(t *testing.T) {
   134→					strings := []string{"a1", "a2", "a3", "a4", "a5"}
   135→					for _, key := range strings {
   136→						out, err := c.GetString(key)
   137→						assert.Nil(t, err)
   138→						assert.Equal(t, "a", out, key)
   139→					}
   140→
   141→					ints := []string{"11", "12", "13", "14", "15", "16"}
   142→					for _, key := range ints {
   143→						out, err := c.GetInt(key)
   144→						require.NoError(t, err)
   145→						assert.Equal(t, 1, out)
   146→					}
   147→
   148→					floats := []string{"21", "22", "23"}
   149→					for _, key := range floats {
   150→						out, err := c.GetFloat(key)
   151→						require.NoError(t, err)
   152→						assert.True(t, 2.0 < out)
   153→					}
   154→
   155→					out, err := c.GetBool("true")
   156→					require.NoError(t, err)
   157→					assert.Equal(t, true, out)
   158→
   159→					out, err = c.GetBool("false")
   160→					require.NoError(t, err)
   161→					assert.Equal(t, false, out)
   162→
   163→					attrs, err := GetAttributes(c, "attr_1")
   164→					require.NoError(t, err)
   165→					assert.Equal(t, tcell.ColorBlue, attrs.Fg)
   166→					assert.Equal(t, tcell.GetColor("#f0f0f0").String(), attrs.Bg.String())
   167→
   168→					for _, key := range []string{"attr_2", "attr_3"} {
   169→						attrs, err = GetAttributes(c, key)
   170→						require.NoError(t, err)
   171→						assert.Equal(t, tcell.ColorRed, attrs.Fg)
   172→						assert.True(t, attrs.Attrs&tcell.AttrBold != 0)
   173→						assert.Equal(t, tcell.ColorDefault, attrs.Bg)
   174→					}
   175→
   176→					duration, err := GetDuration(c, "duration_1", 1*time.Second)
   177→					require.NoError(t, err)
   178→					assert.Equal(t, 2*time.Second, duration)
   179→
   180→					duration, err = GetDuration(c, "duration_2", 1*time.Second)
   181→					require.NoError(t, err)
   182→					assert.Equal(t, 1*time.Second, duration)
   183→				})
   184→			}
   185→		})
   186→	}
   187→}
   188→

    15→package component
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"context"
    19→	"errors"
    20→	"sync"
    21→	"testing"
    22→
    23→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    24→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/component/comptest"
    25→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/term"
    26→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/tui"
    27→	"github.com/unstablebuild/tcell/v3"
    28→)
    29→
    30→func TestAsync(t *testing.T) {
    31→	t.Run("draws placeholder if contstructor is not done", func(t *testing.T) {
    32→		interrupt := term.NopInterrupter()
    33→		a := Async[tui.Component](interrupt, &TestComponent{Ch: 'a'},
    34→			func() (tui.Component, error) {
    35→				<-t.Context().Done()
    36→				return &TestComponent{Ch: 'x'}, nil
    37→			})
    38→		a.Resize(20, 9)
    39→		w := term.NewStringWriter(20, 9)
    40→		tests := []comptest.TestCase{
    41→			{
    42→				Action: nil, Expected: `
    43→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    44→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    45→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    46→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    47→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    48→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    49→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    50→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
    51→aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa`,
    52→			},
    53→		}
    54→		comptest.TestComponent(t, a, w, tests)
    55→	})
    56→
    57→	t.Run("draws constructed component if done", func(t *testing.T) {
    58→		var sema sync.Mutex
    59→		sema.Lock()
    60→		interrupt := term.FuncInterrupter(func(context.Context) error {
    61→			sema.Unlock()
    62→			return nil
    63→		})
    64→		a := Async[tui.Component](interrupt, &TestComponent{Ch: 'a'},
    65→			func() (tui.Component, error) {
    66→				return &TestComponent{Ch: 'x'}, nil
    67→			})
    68→		a.Resize(8, 8)
    69→		w := term.NewStringWriter(20, 9)
    70→		tests := []comptest.TestCase{
    71→			{
    72→				Action: nil, Expected: `
    73→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    74→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    75→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    76→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    77→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    78→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    79→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
    80→xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

    13→// limitations under the License.
    14→
    15→package inputbox
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"testing"
    19→
    20→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    21→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    22→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/component"
    23→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/handler/handlertest"
    24→	"github.com/unstablebuild/rune-go-sdk/term"
    25→	"github.com/unstablebuild/tcell/v3"
    26→)
    27→
    28→func TestHandler(t *testing.T) {
    29→	t.Run("basic input", func(t *testing.T) {
    30→		ib := New()
    31→		cases := []handlertest.SequenceTestCase{
    32→			{InputSequence: "", Expected: "▐         "},
    33→			{InputSequence: "h", Expected: "h▐        "},
    34→			{InputSequence: "e", Expected: "he▐       "},
    35→			{InputSequence: "l", Expected: "hel▐      "},
    36→			{InputSequence: "l", Expected: "hell▐     "},
    37→			{InputSequence: "o", Expected: "hello▐    "},
    38→		}
    39→		handlertest.RunHandlerSequence(t, ib, 10, 1, cases)
    40→	})
    41→
    42→	t.Run("basic input + unknown modifier does nothing", func(t *testing.T) {
    43→		ib := New()
    44→		cases := []handlertest.SequenceTestCase{
    45→			{InputSequence: "<m-c>", Expected: "▐         "},
    46→			{InputSequence: "<m-space>", Expected: "▐         "},
    47→		}
    48→		handlertest.RunHandlerSequence(t, ib, 10, 1, cases)
    49→	})
    50→
    51→	t.Run("input wraps", func(t *testing.T) {
    52→		ib := New()
    53→		cases := []handlertest.SequenceTestCase{
    54→			{InputSequence: "hello", Expected: "hello\n▐    \n     "},
    55→			{InputSequence: "w", Expected: "hello\nw▐   \n     "},
    56→			{InputSequence: "orld", Expected: "hello\nworld\n▐    "},
    57→			{InputSequence: "!", Expected: "hello\nworld\n!▐   "},
    58→		}
    59→		handlertest.RunHandlerSequence(t, ib, 5, 3, cases)
    60→	})
    61→
    62→	t.Run("height with resize", func(t *testing.T) {
    63→		ib := New()
    64→		cases := []handlertest.SequenceTestCase{
    65→			{InputSequence: "helloworld", Expected: "world\n▐    "},
    66→		}
    67→		handlertest.RunHandlerSequence(t, ib, 5, 2, cases)
    68→		actualHeight := ib.Height(5)
    69→		// 10 chars needs 2 lines, but cursor is wrapped at the next one so Height
    70→		// should return 3; thus we render the text in the first and second
    71→		// line and render the cursor at the last one.
    72→		require.Equal(t, 3, actualHeight)
    73→		cases = []handlertest.SequenceTestCase{
    74→			{InputSequence: "helloworl", Expected: "hello\nworld\nhello\nworl▐"},
    75→		}
    76→		handlertest.RunHandlerSequence(t, ib, 5, 4, cases)
    77→		actualHeight = ib.Height(5)
    78→		require.Equal(t, 4, actualHeight) // 19 chars needs 4 lines
    79→	})
    80→}

    38→// stubLSP is a configurable test double for semanticapi.LSP.
    39→// Each field is a function that, when set, overrides the default behaviour.
    40→// Default behaviour returns zero values (happy path) so tests only need to
    41→// configure the methods they care about.
    42→type stubLSP struct {
    43→	onInitialize                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.InitializeParams) (semanticapi.InitializeResult, error)
    44→	onInitialized                 func(context.Context) error
    45→	onShutdown                    func(context.Context) error
    46→	onExit                        func(context.Context) error

    49→	onDidClose                    func(context.Context, semanticapi.DidCloseTextDocumentParams) error
    50→	onDidSave                     func(context.Context, semanticapi.DidSaveTextDocumentParams) error
    51→	onCompletion                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.CompletionParams) (semanticapi.CompletionResult, error)

    53→	onSignatureHelp               func(context.Context, semanticapi.SignatureHelpParams) (*semanticapi.SignatureHelp, error)
    54→	onDefinition                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.DefinitionParams) ([]semanticapi.Location, error)
    55→	onDeclaration                 func(context.Context, semanticapi.DeclarationParams) ([]semanticapi.Location, error)
    56→	onTypeDefinition              func(context.Context, semanticapi.TypeDefinitionParams) ([]semanticapi.Location, error)
    57→	onImplementation              func(context.Context, semanticapi.ImplementationParams) ([]semanticapi.Location, error)
    58→	onReferences                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.ReferenceParams) ([]semanticapi.Location, error)
    59→	onDocumentHighlight           func(context.Context, semanticapi.DocumentHighlightParams) ([]semanticapi.DocumentHighlight, error)
    60→	onDocumentSymbol              func(context.Context, semanticapi.DocumentSymbolParams) ([]semanticapi.DocumentSymbol, error)
    61→	onCodeAction                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.CodeActionParams) ([]semanticapi.CodeAction, error)
    62→	onCodeLens                    func(context.Context, semanticapi.CodeLensParams) ([]semanticapi.CodeLens, error)
    63→	onFormatting                  func(context.Context, semanticapi.DocumentFormattingParams) ([]semanticapi.TextEdit, error)

1278→func newE2EEnv(t *testing.T) *e2eEnv {
  1279→	t.Helper()
  1280→
  1281→	goplsBin, err := exec.LookPath("gopls")
  1282→	if err != nil {
  1283→		for _, p := range []string{
  1284→			filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".rune", "bin", "gopls"),
  1285→			filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), "go", "bin", "gopls"),
  1286→		} {
  1287→			if _, err := os.Stat(p); err == nil {
  1288→				goplsBin = p
  1289→				break
  1290→			}
  1291→		}
  1292→		if goplsBin == "" {
  1293→			t.Skip("gopls not found, skipping e2e tests")
  1294→		}
  1295→	}
  1296→
  1297→	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
  1298→	testdataDir := filepath.Join("testdata")
  1299→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(testdataDir)
  1300→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1301→	for _, e := range entries {
  1302→		src := filepath.Join(testdataDir, e.Name())
  1303→		dst := filepath.Join(tmpDir, e.Name())
  1304→		data, err := os.ReadFile(src)
  1305→		require.NoError(t, err)
  1306→		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(dst, data, 0644))
  1307→	}
  1308→
  1309→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
  1310→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, goplsBin, "serve")
  1311→	cmd.Dir = tmpDir
  1312→	stdinPipe, err := cmd.StdinPipe()
  1313→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1314→	stdoutPipe, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
  1315→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1316→	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
  1317→
  1318→	conn := newRPCConn(stdinPipe, stdoutPipe)
  1319→	adapter := &goplsLSP{conn: conn}
  1320→
  1321→	n := sockCounter.Add(1)
  1322→	sock := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), fmt.Sprintf("lsp_e2e_%d_%d.sock", os.Getpid(), n))
  1323→	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.Remove(sock) })
  1324→	lis, err := net.Listen("unix", sock)
  1325→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1326→
  1327→	srv := grpc.NewServer()
  1328→	semanticrpc.RegisterLSPServer(srv, semanticrpc.NewServer(adapter))
  1329→	go func() { _ = srv.Serve(lis) }()
  1330→
  1331→	cc, err := grpc.NewClient(
  1332→		"passthrough:///unix://"+sock,
  1333→		grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),
  1334→		grpc.WithContextDialer(func(ctx context.Context, addr string) (net.Conn, error) {
  1335→			var d net.Dialer
  1336→			return d.DialContext(ctx, "unix", sock)
  1337→		}),
  1338→	)
  1339→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1340→
  1341→	client := semanticrpc.NewClient(context.Background(), cc)
  1342→
  1343→	rootURI := "file://" + tmpDir
  1344→	mainURI := "file://" + filepath.Join(tmpDir, "main.go")
  1345→	utilURI := "file://" + filepath.Join(tmpDir, "util.go")
  1346→
  1347→	_, err = client.Initialize(ctx, semanticapi.InitializeParams{RootURI: rootURI})
  1348→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1349→	require.NoError(t, client.Initialized(ctx))
  1350→
  1351→	mainContent, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(tmpDir, "main.go"))
  1352→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1353→	utilContent, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(tmpDir, "util.go"))
  1354→	require.NoError(t, err)
  1355→
  1356→	require.NoError(t, client.DidOpen(ctx, semanticapi.DidOpenTextDocumentParams{
  1357→		TextDocument: semanticapi.TextDocumentItem{
  1358→			URI: mainURI, LanguageID: "go", Version: 1, Text: string(mainContent),
  1359→		},
  1360→	}))
  1361→	require.NoError(t, client.DidOpen(ctx, semanticapi.DidOpenTextDocumentParams{
  1362→		TextDocument: semanticapi.TextDocumentItem{
  1363→			URI: utilURI, LanguageID: "go", Version: 1, Text: string(utilContent),
  1364→		},
  1365→	}))
  1366→
  1367→	time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
  1368→
  1369→	stop := func() {
  1370→		_ = client.Shutdown(context.Background())
  1371→		_ = client.Exit(context.Background())
  1372→		_ = cc.Close()
  1373→		srv.GracefulStop()
  1374→		cancel()
  1375→		_ = cmd.Wait()
  1376→	}
  1377→
  1378→	return &e2eEnv{
  1379→		client:  client,
  1380→		mainURI: mainURI,
  1381→		utilURI: utilURI,
  1382→		stop:    stop,
  1383→	}
  1384→}
  1385→

   345→
   346→type jsonrpcMessage struct {
   347→	JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
   348→	ID      *int            `json:"id,omitempty"`
   349→	Method  string          `json:"method,omitempty"`
   350→	Params  json.RawMessage `json:"params,omitempty"`
   351→	Result  json.RawMessage `json:"result,omitempty"`
   352→	Error   *jsonrpcError   `json:"error,omitempty"`
   353→}
   354→
   355→type jsonrpcError struct {
   356→	Code    int    `json:"code"`
   357→	Message string `json:"message"`
   358→}
   359→
   360→type rpcConn struct {
   361→	mu      sync.Mutex
   362→	nextID  int
   363→	stdin   io.WriteCloser
   364→	pending map[int]chan jsonrpcMessage
   365→	closed  chan struct{}
   366→	readErr error
   367→}
   368→
   369→func newRPCConn(stdin io.WriteCloser, stdout io.ReadCloser) *rpcConn {
   370→	c := &rpcConn{
   371→		stdin:   stdin,
   372→		pending: make(map[int]chan jsonrpcMessage),
   373→		closed:  make(chan struct{}),
   374→	}
   375→	go c.readLoop(stdout)
   376→	return c
   377→}
   378→
   379→func (c *rpcConn) readLoop(r io.Reader) {
   380→	br := bufio.NewReader(r)
   381→	for {
   382→		var contentLen int
   383→		for {
   384→			line, err := br.ReadString('\n')
   385→			if err != nil {
   386→				c.mu.Lock()
   387→				c.readErr = err
   388→				for _, ch := range c.pending {
   389→					close(ch)
   390→				}
   391→				c.mu.Unlock()
   392→				close(c.closed)
   393→				return
   394→			}
   395→			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   396→			if line == "" {
   397→				break
   398→			}
   399→			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "Content-Length:") {
   400→				val := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "Content-Length:"))
   401→				contentLen, _ = strconv.Atoi(val)
   402→			}
   403→		}
   404→		if contentLen == 0 {
   405→			continue
   406→		}
   407→
   408→		body := make([]byte, contentLen)
   409→		if _, err := io.ReadFull(br, body); err != nil {
   410→			c.mu.Lock()
   411→			c.readErr = err
   412→			for _, ch := range c.pending {
   413→				close(ch)
   414→			}
   415→			c.mu.Unlock()
   416→			close(c.closed)
   417→			return
   418→		}
   419→
   420→		var msg jsonrpcMessage
   421→		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &msg); err != nil {
   422→			continue
   423→		}
   424→
   425→		if msg.ID != nil && msg.Method == "" {
   426→			c.mu.Lock()
   427→			ch, ok := c.pending[*msg.ID]
   428→			if ok {
   429→				delete(c.pending, *msg.ID)
   430→			}
   431→			c.mu.Unlock()
   432→			if ok {
   433→				ch <- msg
   434→			}
   435→		}
   436→	}
   437→}
   438→
   439→func (c *rpcConn) call(ctx context.Context, method string, params, result any) error {
   440→	c.mu.Lock()
   441→	id := c.nextID
   442→	c.nextID++
   443→	ch := make(chan jsonrpcMessage, 1)
   444→	c.pending[id] = ch
   445→	c.mu.Unlock()
   446→
   447→	paramBytes, err := json.Marshal(params)
   448→	if err != nil {
   449→		return err
   450→	}
   451→	req := jsonrpcMessage{
   452→		JSONRPC: "2.0",
   453→		ID:      &id,
   454→		Method:  method,
   455→		Params:  paramBytes,
   456→	}
   457→	body, err := json.Marshal(req)
   458→	if err != nil {
   459→		return err
   460→	}
   461→	header := fmt.Sprintf("Content-Length: %d\r\n\r\n", len(body))
   462→
   463→	c.mu.Lock()
   464→	_, err = io.WriteString(c.stdin, header)
   465→	if err == nil {
   466→		_, err = c.stdin.Write(body)
   467→	}
   468→	c.mu.Unlock()
   469→	if err != nil {
   470→		return err
   471→	}
   472→
   473→	select {
   474→	case <-ctx.Done():
   475→		return ctx.Err()
   476→	case msg, ok := <-ch:
   477→		if !ok {
   478→			return fmt.Errorf("connection closed: %v", c.readErr)
   479→		}
   480→		if msg.Error != nil {
   481→			return fmt.Errorf("rpc error %d: %s", msg.Error.Code, msg.Error.Message)
   482→		}
   483→		if result != nil && msg.Result != nil {
   484→			return json.Unmarshal(msg.Result, result)
   485→		}
   486→		return nil
   487→	}
   488→}
   489→
   490→func (c *rpcConn) notify(ctx context.Context, method string, params any) error {
   491→	paramBytes, err := json.Marshal(params)
   492→	if err != nil {
   493→		return err
   494→	}
   495→	req := jsonrpcMessage{
   496→		JSONRPC: "2.0",
   497→		Method:  method,
   498→		Params:  paramBytes,
   499→	}
   500→	body, err := json.Marshal(req)
   501→	if err != nil {
   502→		return err
   503→	}
   504→	header := fmt.Sprintf("Content-Length: %d\r\n\r\n", len(body))
   505→
   506→	c.mu.Lock()
   507→	defer c.mu.Unlock()
   508→	if _, err := io.WriteString(c.stdin, header); err != nil {
   509→		return err
   510→	}
   511→	_, err = c.stdin.Write(body)
   512→	return err
   513→}

   593→func lspPosToSemantic(p lspPosition) semanticapi.Position {
   594→	return semanticapi.Position{Line: p.Line, Character: p.Character}
   595→}
   596→
   597→func lspRangeToSemantic(r lspRange) semanticapi.Range {
   598→	return semanticapi.Range{Start: lspPosToSemantic(r.Start), End: lspPosToSemantic(r.End)}
   599→}
   600→
   601→func lspTextEditToSemantic(e lspTextEdit) semanticapi.TextEdit {
   602→	return semanticapi.TextEdit{Range: lspRangeToSemantic(e.Range), NewText: e.NewText}
   603→}
   604→
   605→func lspMarkupToSemantic(m lspMarkupContent) semanticapi.MarkupContent {
   606→	kind := semanticapi.MarkupKindPlainText
   607→	if m.Kind == "markdown" {
   608→		kind = semanticapi.MarkupKindMarkdown
   609→	}
   610→	return semanticapi.MarkupContent{Kind: kind, Value: m.Value}
   611→}
   612→
   613→func lspWorkspaceEditToSemantic(e *lspWorkspaceEdit) *semanticapi.WorkspaceEdit {
   614→	if e == nil {
   615→		return nil
   616→	}
   617→	ret := &semanticapi.WorkspaceEdit{Changes: make(map[string][]semanticapi.TextEdit)}
   618→	for uri, edits := range e.Changes {
   619→		for _, te := range edits {
   620→			ret.Changes[uri] = append(ret.Changes[uri], lspTextEditToSemantic(te))
   621→		}
   622→	}
   623→	for _, dc := range e.DocumentChanges {
   624→		uri := dc.TextDocument.URI
   625→		for _, te := range dc.Edits {
   626→			ret.Changes[uri] = append(ret.Changes[uri], lspTextEditToSemantic(te))
   627→		}
   628→	}
   629→	return ret
   630→}
   631→
   632→func lspCompletionItemToSemantic(item lspCompletionItem) semanticapi.CompletionItem {
   633→	ret := semanticapi.CompletionItem{
   634→		Label:            item.Label,
   635→		Kind:             semanticapi.CompletionItemKind(item.Kind),
   636→		Detail:           item.Detail,
   637→		InsertText:       item.InsertText,
   638→		InsertTextFormat: semanticapi.InsertTextFormat(item.InsertTextFormat),
   639→	}
   640→	if item.Documentation != nil {
   641→		var mc lspMarkupContent
   642→		if err := json.Unmarshal(item.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   643→			v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   644→			ret.Documentation = &v
   645→		} else {
   646→			var s string
   647→			if err := json.Unmarshal(item.Documentation, &s); err == nil && s != "" {
   648→				ret.Documentation = &semanticapi.MarkupContent{Kind: semanticapi.MarkupKindPlainText, Value: s}
   649→			}
   650→		}
   651→	}
   652→	if item.TextEdit != nil {
   653→		te := lspTextEditToSemantic(*item.TextEdit)
   654→		ret.TextEdit = &te
   655→	}
   656→	return ret
   657→}
   658→
   659→func lspSignatureHelpToSemantic(sh *lspSignatureHelp) *semanticapi.SignatureHelp {
   660→	if sh == nil {
   661→		return nil
   662→	}
   663→	ret := &semanticapi.SignatureHelp{
   664→		ActiveSignature: sh.ActiveSignature,
   665→		ActiveParameter: sh.ActiveParameter,
   666→	}
   667→	for _, sig := range sh.Signatures {
   668→		si := semanticapi.SignatureInformation{Label: sig.Label}
   669→		if sig.Documentation != nil {
   670→			var mc lspMarkupContent
   671→			if err := json.Unmarshal(sig.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   672→				v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   673→				si.Documentation = &v
   674→			}
   675→		}
   676→		for _, p := range sig.Parameters {
   677→			pi := semanticapi.ParameterInformation{}
   678→			// Label can be a string or [start, end] tuple.
   679→			var labelStr string
   680→			if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Label, &labelStr); err == nil {
   681→				pi.Label = labelStr
   682→			} else {
   683→				var labelArr [2]int
   684→				if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Label, &labelArr); err == nil {
   685→					pi.Label = sig.Label[labelArr[0]:labelArr[1]]
   686→				}
   687→			}
   688→			if p.Documentation != nil {
   689→				var mc lspMarkupContent
   690→				if err := json.Unmarshal(p.Documentation, &mc); err == nil && mc.Value != "" {
   691→					v := lspMarkupToSemantic(mc)
   692→					pi.Documentation = &v
   693→				}
   694→			}
   695→			si.Parameters = append(si.Parameters, pi)
   696→		}
   697→		ret.Signatures = append(ret.Signatures, si)
   698→	}
   699→	return ret
   700→}

   708→func (g *goplsLSP) Initialize(ctx context.Context, params semanticapi.InitializeParams) (semanticapi.InitializeResult, error) {
   709→	type initParams struct {

   730→				},
   731→				"selectionRange":    map[string]any{},
   732→				"documentHighlight": map[string]any{},

   734→				"semanticTokens": map[string]any{
   735→					"requests": map[string]any{

   738→					},
   739→					"tokenTypes":     []string{"namespace", "type", "class", "enum", "interface", "struct", "typeParameter", "parameter", "variable", "property", "enumMember", "event", "function", "method", "macro", "keyword", "modifier", "comment", "string", "number", "regexp", "operator", "decorator", "label"},
   740→					"tokenModifiers": []string{"declaration", "definition", "readonly", "static", "deprecated", "abstract", "async", "modification", "documentation", "defaultLibrary"},
   741→					"formats":        []string{"relative"},
   742→				},
   743→			},
   744→			"workspace": map[string]any{

   749→	var raw json.RawMessage

   892→func (g *goplsLSP) CodeAction(ctx context.Context, params semanticapi.CodeActionParams) ([]semanticapi.CodeAction, error) {
   893→	diags := make([]any, len(params.Context.Diagnostics))
   894→	for i, d := range params.Context.Diagnostics {
   895→		diags[i] = map[string]any{
   896→			"range":    d.Range,
   897→			"severity": int(d.Severity),
   898→			"message":  d.Message,
   899→		}
   900→	}
   901→	p := map[string]any{
   902→		"textDocument": map[string]any{"uri": params.TextDocument.URI},
   903→		"range":        params.Range,
   904→		"context":      map[string]any{"diagnostics": diags},
   905→	}
   906→	var result []lspCodeAction
   907→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, "textDocument/codeAction", p, &result); err != nil {
   908→		return nil, err
   909→	}
   910→	ret := make([]semanticapi.CodeAction, len(result))
   911→	for i, a := range result {
   912→		ret[i] = semanticapi.CodeAction{
   913→			Title: a.Title,
   914→			Kind:  semanticapi.CodeActionKind(a.Kind),
   915→			Edit:  lspWorkspaceEditToSemantic(a.Edit),
   916→		}
   917→		if a.Command != nil {
   918→			cmd := &semanticapi.Command{
   919→				Title:   a.Command.Title,
   920→				Command: a.Command.Command,
   921→			}
   922→			cmd.Arguments = append(cmd.Arguments, a.Command.Arguments...)
   923→			ret[i].Command = cmd
   924→		}
   925→	}
   926→	return ret, nil
   927→}

  1078→func (g *goplsLSP) ExecuteCommand(ctx context.Context, params semanticapi.ExecuteCommandParams) (string, error) {

  1083→	p := map[string]any{

  1085→		"arguments": args,
  1086→	}
  1087→	var raw json.RawMessage
  1088→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, "workspace/executeCommand", p, &raw); err != nil {
  1089→		return "", err
  1090→	}
  1091→	if raw == nil {

  1251→func (g *goplsLSP) locationRequest(ctx context.Context, method string, params any) ([]semanticapi.Location, error) {
  1252→	var raw json.RawMessage
  1253→	if err := g.conn.call(ctx, method, params, &raw); err != nil {
  1254→		return nil, err
  1255→	}
  1256→	if raw == nil || string(raw) == "null" {
  1257→		return nil, nil
  1258→	}
  1259→	// gopls may return a single Location or []Location.
  1260→	var locs []semanticapi.Location
  1261→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &locs); err != nil {
  1262→		var single semanticapi.Location
  1263→		if err2 := json.Unmarshal(raw, &single); err2 != nil {
  1264→			return nil, err
  1265→		}
  1266→		locs = []semanticapi.Location{single}
  1267→	}
  1268→	return locs, nil
  1269→}
